meta_commentary:
This plan threads a new `timeline_placement` contract through 4 layers: frontend selection → agent tool → task resolver persistence → completion-time insertion. The critical persistence boundary is in the `create-task` resolvers — `buildTaskParams()` in `imageGeneration.ts` uses an explicit whitelist that will silently drop unknown fields. The plan addresses this via `setTaskLineageFields()` in `lineage.ts`, which already has precedent in `magicEdit.ts` and `zImageTurboI2I.ts`.

Execution ordering matters: land the contract + lineage extension first (T1-T2), then wire the agent tool layer (T3-T4), then complete_task consumption (T5-T6), then tests (T7-T9). The prompt updates in T4 should be conservative — the model needs to know the schema field exists but shouldn't over-specify placement for every request.

Key gotcha: `imageGeneration.ts` does NOT currently call `setTaskLineageFields` at all, unlike magicEdit and zImageTurboI2I. The executor must add that call after `buildTaskParams` returns, matching the pattern in the other resolvers. `buildQueuedTask()` passes params through without filtering, so once lineage sets the field, it survives to the task row.

For `complete_task`, the plan reuses existing timeline mutation helpers from `tools/timeline.ts` and versioned save from `db.ts`. The executor should import `addMediaClip()` and `saveTimelineConfigVersioned()` rather than writing a new insertion path. If the timeline mutation fails after generation creation, the task should still complete — log the placement failure as a follow-up issue, don't lose the output asset.

The frontend changes (Step 1) are the least risky — they extend existing types and add fields to the attachment mapping in AgentChat.tsx. The server-side enrichment in selectedClips.ts needs timeline state that's already loaded in the agent loop.

tasks.description: Define the timeline_placement contract and extend TaskLineageFields in lineage.ts. Add a `timelinePlacement` field to `TaskLineageFields` (type: `{ timeline_id: string; source_clip_id: string; target_track: string; insertion_time: number; intent: 'after_source' | 'replace' }` or similar minimal shape). Update `setTaskLineageFields()` to write `timeline_placement` into the payload when present. This is the foundation — all downstream persistence depends on this field existing in lineage.
tasks.executor_notes: Added an exported `TimelinePlacement` contract in `lineage.ts`, extended `TaskLineageFields` with `timelinePlacement`, and verified by inspection that `setTaskLineageFields()` now writes the snake_case payload key `timeline_placement` when present.

tasks.description: Wire setTaskLineageFields into imageGeneration resolver. Currently `imageGeneration.ts` does NOT call `setTaskLineageFields` — unlike magicEdit.ts:104 and zImageTurboI2I.ts:111 which already do. Add the call after `buildTaskParams()` returns (~line 227), matching the exact pattern in magicEdit.ts. Also verify that magicEdit.ts and zImageTurboI2I.ts pass the new `timelinePlacement` field from their input through to `setTaskLineageFields`. The input object for each resolver comes from the `create-task` edge function request body, so the field must be accepted there too.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated all three resolver inputs to accept `timeline_placement`, passed that field into `setTaskLineageFields`, and added the missing `imageGeneration.ts` lineage hook immediately after `buildTaskParams()` returns. Verification: code inspection confirms `imageGeneration.ts` now calls `setTaskLineageFields()` post-`buildTaskParams`, and `magicEdit.ts` / `zImageTurboI2I.ts` now forward `input.timeline_placement` into `timelinePlacement`. There is still no resolver-level placement test yet; that remains future T6 work. Broader create-task edge suites were rerun, but the current failures are pre-existing and unrelated to `timeline_placement` threading (`index.test.ts` expects the old family-less request shape; `dispatch.test.ts` has a broken mocked `__resetServeHandler`).

tasks.description: Extend frontend attachment types and stop dropping selection metadata. (1) Add timeline-specific fields to `SelectedMediaClip` in useSelectedMediaClips.ts and `AgentTurnAttachment` in agent-session.ts: at minimum `trackId`, `at` (start time), `duration`, and `isTimelineBacked` boolean. (2) In AgentChat.tsx handleSend (~line 387), include these new fields in the attachment mapping instead of dropping them. (3) Extend `SelectedClipPayload` in supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/types.ts with matching server-side fields: `track_id`, `at`, `duration`, `is_timeline_backed`.
tasks.executor_notes: Extended `SelectedMediaClip`, `AgentTurnAttachment`, and `SelectedClipPayload` with timeline metadata (`trackId/track_id`, `at`, `duration`, `isTimelineBacked/is_timeline_backed`). Preserved those fields through merged selection handling, `AgentChat` attachment mapping, frontend invocation payload building, edge normalization, and stored-turn recovery. Verified with frontend Vitest (`useAgentSession.test.tsx`, `AgentChat.test.tsx`) and edge Vitest (`selectedClips.test.ts`, `loop.test.ts`). The first frontend run exposed stale summary expectations after whole-shot metadata started surviving the merge; tests were updated and the full affected suites were rerun successfully.

tasks.description: Add server-side selection enrichment and thread placement through the agent tool layer. (1) In selectedClips.ts, add a `resolveTimelinePlacement()` helper that takes a single timeline-backed clip and the loaded timeline state, returning a placement object with track, insertion time (source.at + source.duration), and intent. (2) In loop.ts (~line 419), after `loadTimelineState`, call the resolver to enrich selected clips with deterministic placement facts from the loaded config. (3) Update prompts.ts so the system prompt includes structured selection context with the 'after source clip' anchor when a single timeline clip is eligible. (4) Add `timeline_placement` to the create_task tool schema in tool-schemas.ts as an optional object parameter. (5) In tools/create-task.ts, read the `timeline_placement` arg and pass it through the `CreateGenerationTaskArgs` in generation.ts → `buildCreateTaskRequest()` so it lands in the task creation request body. (6) Update prompts.ts instructions so the model knows to include placement intent on create_task when the user asks to edit-and-insert.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `resolveTimelinePlacement()` in `selectedClips.ts`, enriched selected clips after `loadTimelineState()` in `runAgentLoop()`, persisted the resolved placement on stored turn attachments for recovery, exposed the placement anchor conservatively in `prompts.ts`, added `timeline_placement` to the `create_task` schema, validated it in `tools/create-task.ts`, and threaded it through `CreateGenerationTaskArgs` into `tools/generation.ts` so `buildCreateTaskRequest()` includes it in the create-task request input. Verification: focused edge suites passed for `selectedClips.test.ts`, `loop.test.ts`, and `tools/create-task.test.ts`; frontend attachment/session suites also passed after the shared attachment type gained `timelinePlacement`.

tasks.description: Implement completion-time placement in complete_task. (1) In params.ts, add `extractTimelinePlacement(params)` that reads and validates the `timeline_placement` object from task params, returning null if absent or malformed. (2) In handler.ts, after generation creation succeeds (~line 210+), check for stored placement intent via the new extractor. If present, load the timeline config, call the existing `addMediaClip()` helper from tools/timeline.ts to insert the completed asset at the stored insertion point, then persist via `saveTimelineConfigVersioned()` from db.ts. (3) If the timeline mutation fails after generation creation, log the error and surface it as a follow-up issue — do NOT fail the task or lose the output asset. This matches the existing complete_task pattern of preserving primary completion.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `extractTimelinePlacement()` in `params.ts`, then wired `complete_task` to attempt placement only after a created generation returns a usable id. The handler now loads the target timeline state, registers the completed asset in the timeline registry, inserts it with `addMediaClip()`, saves via `saveTimelineConfigVersioned()`, and records a `timeline_placement_failed` follow-up issue instead of failing completion if any placement step errors. Verification: full `supabase/functions/complete_task/params.test.ts` and `supabase/functions/complete_task/handler.test.ts` passed under the edge Vitest config.

tasks.description: Add resolver-level tests under resolvers/__tests__/ that exercise the real imageGenerationResolver, magicEditResolver, and zImageTurboI2I resolver with a `timeline_placement` field in the input and assert that the stored task params contain the placement object. This is the persistence boundary where placement is most likely to be silently dropped. Pattern: construct a realistic resolver input with timeline_placement set, call the resolver, inspect the returned params object. These tests should run first in validation.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `resolvers/__tests__/placement.test.ts` that calls the real `imageGenerationResolver`, `magicEditResolver`, and `zImageTurboI2IResolver` with a realistic `timeline_placement` object and asserts the returned task params still contain that object. Verification: the full placement test file passed under the edge Vitest config.

tasks.description: Add focused unit tests for attachment forwarding, selection resolution, and prompt enrichment. (1) In useAgentSession.test.tsx: test that timeline-specific fields (trackId, at, duration, isTimelineBacked) survive through the attachment pipeline. (2) In AgentChat.test.tsx: test that the new fields are included in sent attachments. (3) In selectedClips.test.ts: test the new `resolveTimelinePlacement()` helper with timeline-backed and gallery-only clips. (4) In loop.test.ts: test that buildSelectedClipsPrompt includes the structured placement anchor when a single timeline clip is selected.
tasks.executor_notes: Extended the focused test surface for timeline-aware attachments and prompts: frontend tests now assert timeline attachment coordinates survive through `useSendMessage` and `AgentChat` send payloads, `selectedClips.test.ts` explicitly covers both timeline-backed placement resolution and gallery-only null behavior, and `loop.test.ts` now asserts a single selected timeline clip yields a structured `placement_anchor` in the prompt. Verification: full frontend and edge suites passed.

tasks.description: Add task-path tests for placement intent threading and completion-time insertion. (1) In create-task.test.ts: test that executeCreateTask passes timeline_placement through when the agent specifies it, and omits it when not specified. (2) In handler.test.ts: test that completeTaskHandler reads placement intent from task params and attempts timeline insertion when present, and that it still completes the task successfully if timeline insertion fails. (3) Add one end-to-end harness case in cases.ts for 'edit selected image and insert result after the source clip' with real task completion enabled.
tasks.executor_notes: Added create-task tests that assert `executeCreateTask()` forwards `timeline_placement` when present and omits it when absent, added complete_task handler tests that verify timeline insertion is attempted for stored placement intent and that completion still succeeds with a degraded follow-up when insertion fails, and added a harness case that selects a timeline-backed clip and expects task params to include `timeline_placement` plus downstream generation and timeline insertion effects. Verification: full `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.test.ts`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/handler.test.ts`, and smoke `supabase/functions/_tests/harness/harness.test.ts` passed.

tasks.description: Run all affected test suites and verify everything passes. Run in this order per the validation plan: (1) resolver-level tests: `npx vitest run supabase/functions/create-task/resolvers/__tests__/placement.test.ts` (2) selectedClips and loop tests: `npx vitest run supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/selectedClips.test.ts supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.test.ts` (3) create-task and handler tests: `npx vitest run supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.test.ts supabase/functions/complete_task/handler.test.ts` (4) frontend tests: `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.test.tsx` (5) broader edge suite: `npm run test:edge:unit`. Fix any failures before completing.

watch_items: PERSISTENCE BOUNDARY: imageGeneration.ts buildTaskParams() uses an explicit whitelist. timeline_placement must be injected via setTaskLineageFields AFTER buildTaskParams returns, not inside it. Verify buildQueuedTask passes enriched params through without filtering.

RESOLVER GAP: imageGeneration.ts currently does NOT call setTaskLineageFields at all — this is the only edit-family resolver missing the call. magicEdit.ts and zImageTurboI2I.ts already have it. The executor must add it, not assume it exists.

COMPLETE_TASK RESILIENCE: If timeline mutation fails after generation creation succeeds, the task must still complete. Do not wrap both in a single try/catch that would lose the output asset. Match existing pattern of preserving primary completion.
TIMELINE STATE LOADING: The agent loop already loads timeline state at loop.ts:419. Selection enrichment should use this already-loaded state, not fetch it again.
VERSIONED SAVE: Timeline insertion in complete_task must use saveTimelineConfigVersioned (optimistic concurrency) from db.ts, not a raw update. Read current config_version before mutation.
REUSE EXISTING HELPERS: Timeline clip insertion must use the existing addMediaClip() from tools/timeline.ts, not a new ad-hoc write path. This is a 'should' success criterion.

FRONTEND TYPE ALIGNMENT: SelectedMediaClip (frontend), AgentTurnAttachment (types), and SelectedClipPayload (backend) must all carry the same timeline-specific fields. Keep naming consistent (camelCase frontend, snake_case backend).
DEBT: Do not worsen any existing debt items. In particular, do not touch pinned shot groups, resolve-overlaps, or undo/history patterns that appear in the debt watch list.
SCOPE: v1 targets single-output tasks from one selected timeline clip only. Do not handle batched count>1, multi-selection, video outputs, or variant placement in this pass.

PROMPT CHANGES: Be conservative with prompt updates. The model needs to know timeline_placement exists in the schema but should not over-specify it for every request. Only include placement when the user explicitly asks for edit-and-insert behavior.

sense_checks.question: Does the new `timelinePlacement` field in TaskLineageFields get written to the task params payload by setTaskLineageFields, and is the field name `timeline_placement` (snake_case) in the stored params?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed in `supabase/functions/create-task/resolvers/shared/lineage.ts` that `TaskLineageFields.timelinePlacement` is accepted and persisted as `payload["timeline_placement"]`.

sense_checks.question: Does imageGeneration.ts now call setTaskLineageFields after buildTaskParams, and do all three resolvers (imageGeneration, magicEdit, zImageTurboI2I) pass timelinePlacement from their input to the lineage call?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed by inspection that `imageGeneration.ts` now calls `setTaskLineageFields()` after `buildTaskParams()` returns, and all three resolvers (`imageGeneration.ts`, `magicEdit.ts`, `zImageTurboI2I.ts`) pass `input.timeline_placement` into the lineage call as `timelinePlacement`.

sense_checks.question: Are the new timeline fields (trackId/track_id, at, duration, isTimelineBacked/is_timeline_backed) present in all three type definitions (SelectedMediaClip, AgentTurnAttachment, SelectedClipPayload) and included in the AgentChat attachment mapping?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed the new fields exist in all three type layers (`SelectedMediaClip`, `AgentTurnAttachment`, `SelectedClipPayload`) and are forwarded in the `AgentChat` attachment mapping. The preservation path was also verified through `useAgentSession.ts`, `selectedClips.ts`, and `loop.ts` plus passing frontend/edge tests.

sense_checks.question: Does the create_task tool schema include timeline_placement as an optional parameter, and does the agent tool threading pass it through generation.ts buildCreateTaskRequest into the task creation HTTP request body?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed by inspection that `tool-schemas.ts` now exposes optional `timeline_placement`, `tools/create-task.ts` reads and validates it, and `tools/generation.ts` includes it in the `buildCreateTaskRequest()` input payload that is POSTed to the create-task edge function. Focused edge tests for selected clip enrichment / loop prompting / create-task tool threading passed.

sense_checks.question: Does complete_task handler attempt timeline insertion only when timeline_placement is present in params, and does it preserve the completed generation even if timeline insertion fails?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed in code and by the full `complete_task` params/handler test run that timeline insertion is only attempted when `extractTimelinePlacement()` returns a valid placement, and any later placement failure is caught into `completionFollowUpIssues` instead of failing the completed task.

sense_checks.question: Do resolver-level tests exercise the REAL resolver functions (not mocks) with timeline_placement in the input and assert it appears in the output params object?
sense_checks.executor_note: Added `supabase/functions/create-task/resolvers/__tests__/placement.test.ts`, which exercises the real resolver functions with `timeline_placement` in their inputs and asserts the returned task params still include that exact placement object.

sense_checks.question: Do the selectedClips tests cover both timeline-backed clips (with placement resolution) and gallery-only clips (no placement anchor)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Updated `selectedClips.test.ts` to cover both timeline-backed placement resolution and gallery-only null behavior, and updated `loop.test.ts` to assert the placement anchor is surfaced when a single selected timeline clip is eligible.

sense_checks.question: Does the harness case in cases.ts exercise the full async path: selected clip → create_task with placement → task completion → timeline insertion?
sense_checks.executor_note: Added a harness case that selects a timeline-backed clip, leaves `skipTaskCompletion` unset so the runner waits for async task side effects, and asserts the resulting task params contain `timeline_placement`, a generation is created, and a media clip is added on the source track after completion.

sense_checks.question: Do all test suites pass, including existing tests that were not modified (regression check)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Not fully. All targeted timeline-placement suites passed, and the temporary repro script confirmed `timeline_placement` survives create-task request construction and completion-side extraction. The broader regression check `npm run test:edge:unit` still fails with 10 failures in five untouched files outside this batch: `supabase/functions/create-task/index.test.ts`, `supabase/functions/create-task/resolvers/__tests__/dispatch.test.ts`, `supabase/functions/_shared/billing.test.ts`, `supabase/functions/ai-prompt/index.test.ts`, and `supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.test.ts`.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Preserve and resolve selection context (stop dropping metadata, extend types, add server-side enrichment, update prompts)
Step 2: Persist explicit post-task placement intent (define contract, thread through agent tool, extend resolver persistence, tighten prompts)
Step 3: Apply placement at task completion (extract/validate helpers in params.ts, consume intent in handler.ts, resilient error handling)
Step 4: Prove the behavior (unit tests for attachments/selection, resolver-level tests, task-path tests, harness case)

validation.completeness_notes: All 4 plan steps are covered. T1-T2 implement the placement contract and resolver persistence (Step 2 core). T3-T4 implement selection context preservation and agent-tool threading (Steps 1+2 threading). T5 implements completion-time placement (Step 3). T6-T9 implement all testing requirements (Step 4). The execution order follows the plan's recommended sequence: contract first, then agent threading, then completion consumption, then tests.
